examples.title: File Copy Operation using System Calls
examples.description: Demonstrates how system programs invoke system calls to perform file operations
examples.code:
PROGRAM FileCopy
BEGIN
    INPUT sourceFile, destinationFile
    
    // Open source file (system call)
    sourceFD = open(sourceFile, READ_ONLY)
    IF sourceFD == -1 THEN
        PRINT "Error: Cannot open source file"
        EXIT
    END IF
    
    // Create/overwrite destination file (system call)
    destFD = open(destinationFile, WRITE_ONLY | CREATE_TRUNCATE)
    IF destFD == -1 THEN
        PRINT "Error: Cannot create destination file"
        close(sourceFD)
        EXIT
    END IF
    
    // Read from source and write to destination in chunks
    WHILE (bytesRead = read(sourceFD, buffer, BUFFER_SIZE)) > 0 DO
        write(destFD, buffer, bytesRead)
    END WHILE
    
    // Close files (system calls)
    close(sourceFD)
    close(destFD)
    
    PRINT "File copied successfully"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how file management system programs work. The copy command invokes system calls (open, read, write, close) which are handled by the kernel to perform actual file operations on the filesystem.

examples.title: Simple Shell Implementation
examples.description: Basic command interpreter that processes user commands
examples.explanation: This Python code implements a basic command interpreter (shell). It demonstrates how shells accept user commands, parse them, and either execute built-in commands or launch external programs using system calls like fork() and exec().

examples.title: System Call Example - Process Information
examples.description: C program demonstrating use of system calls to get process information
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates various system calls used to obtain process and system information. Functions like getpid(), getppid(), getuid(), getcwd(), and sysinfo() are system calls that request information from the OS kernel.

examples.title: Disk Defragmentation Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates how disk maintenance system programs work
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DiskDefragment
INPUT: Disk drive letter or mount point
OUTPUT: Defragmented disk

BEGIN
    // Phase 1: Scan and analyze disk
    PRINT "Analyzing disk..."
    fileList = SCAN_ALL_FILES(disk)
    FOR each file IN fileList DO
        fileInfo = GET_FILE_LOCATION(file)
        IF fileInfo.isFragmented THEN
            ADD to fragmentedFilesList
        END IF
    END FOR
    
    PRINT "Found", COUNT(fragmentedFilesList), "fragmented files"
    
    // Phase 2: Sort files by access frequency (optional optimization)
    SORT fragmentedFilesList BY accessFrequency DESC
    
    // Phase 3: Relocate fragmented files
    freeSpaces = GET_FREE_SPACE_REGIONS(disk)
    currentPosition = START_OF_DATA_AREA
    
    FOR each file IN fragmentedFilesList DO
        // Read file blocks
        blocks = READ_FILE_BLOCKS(file)
        
        // Find continuous space
        FOR each space IN freeSpaces DO
            IF space.size >= blocks.size THEN
                // Move file to continuous space
                WRITE_TO_DISK(blocks, space.location)
                UPDATE_FILE_TABLE(file, space.location)
                REMOVE freeSpaces(space)
                BREAK
            END IF
        END FOR
        
        PRINT "Defragmented:", file.name
    END FOR
    
    // Phase 4: Update filesystem metadata
    UPDATE_FILESYSTEM_TABLES()
    
    PRINT "Disk defragmentation complete"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode illustrates how disk defragmentation utilities work. They scan for fragmented files, identify free spaces, and relocate file blocks to contiguous areas on disk to improve read/write performance.
